mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (measured by GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes) drives positive equity valuations through increased corporate earnings expectations. A sustained positive trend in real demand signals expanding profit margins and justifies higher equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q3 2006 real GDP growth indicates sustained expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 3+ consecutive months, monetary tightening constrains credit availability and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 3+ months, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE increasing by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative equity returns over 5 trading days as the market front-runs earnings downgrades from weakening consumer demand.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE change and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector health. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, it indicates demand destruction across the economy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1772.0K and RSAFS at 363673M as of 2006-09-01
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 5% MoM, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates profit margins through improved capacity utilization and operational efficiency. A sustained 3-month uptrend in INDPRO signals supply-side strength and cost control, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors recognize margin expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO trends are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicate labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 50K/month while UNRATE remains stable or rises, it signals weakening labor demand and future earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 50K and UNRATE stable or rising
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135844K and UNRATE at 4.4% as of 2006-10-01
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 50K and UNRATE stable/rising, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint signal produces lower predictive power than PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) acceleration above 3% year-over-year combined with elevated FEDFUNDS (> 5.0%) signals the Federal Reserve is in active tightening mode to combat price pressures. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market prices in extended monetary restraint and growth deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 202.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2006-10-01
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the joint signal is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) momentum measured as 3-month moving average growth rate directly reflects consumer spending strength and near-term corporate revenue visibility. Positive RSAFS momentum produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market immediately reprices earnings expectations upward based on consumer activity signals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum is followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances.
